informatics.q: What does HL7 stand for in healthcare data exchange?
informatics.a: Health Level Seven International standard
c: Health Level Seven International standard
Hospital Link 7 communication protocol
Healthcare Logistics version 7 standard
Health Laboratory 7 data interchange
informatics.e: HL7 (Health Level Seven) refers to both the standards organization and the messaging standards for exchanging clinical and administrative healthcare data.

informatics.q: What is HL7 FHIR and what does the acronym stand for?
informatics.a: Fast Healthcare Interoperability Resources
c: Fast Healthcare Interoperability Resources
Federal Health Information Repository system
Flexible Healthcare Integration and Routing
Federated Health Interexchange Records base
informatics.e: FHIR is a modern HL7 standard that uses RESTful APIs and common web technologies (JSON, XML, HTTP) to enable healthcare data exchange.

informatics.q: Which data format does FHIR primarily use for resource representation?
informatics.a: JSON and XML web-standard formats
c: JSON and XML web-standard formats
Binary and proprietary encoded formats
HL7 v2 pipe-delimited message format
CSV and tab-delimited flat text files
informatics.e: FHIR resources can be represented in JSON, XML, or RDF (Turtle). JSON is most commonly used in modern implementations due to its simplicity and broad developer familiarity.

informatics.q: In HL7 v2 messaging, what character is used as the field separator?
informatics.a: The pipe character (vertical bar |)
c: The pipe character (vertical bar |)
The comma character as CSV delimiter
The semicolon character as separator
The colon character as field divider
informatics.e: HL7 v2 messages use the pipe character | as the primary field separator. The message header (MSH) segment defines encoding characters.

informatics.q: Which HL7 message type is used for patient admission, discharge, and transfer events?
informatics.a: ADT (Admit Discharge Transfer) messages
c: ADT (Admit Discharge Transfer) messages
ORM (Order Message) transaction request
ORU (Observation Result Unsolicited) type
SIU (Schedule Information Unsolicited) type
informatics.e: ADT messages communicate patient demographic and visit information including admissions, discharges, transfers, and registration events across hospital systems.

informatics.q: What is a key advantage of FHIR over HL7 v2 for system integration?
informatics.a: RESTful API design with standard web protocols
c: RESTful API design with standard web protocols
Higher data compression ratios for storage
Native support for legacy mainframe computers
Built-in real-time video streaming capability
informatics.e: FHIR uses RESTful APIs, OAuth2 authentication, and standard web technologies, making it more developer-friendly and easier to implement than v2 messaging.

informatics.q: What is the purpose of a FHIR 'Bundle' resource?
informatics.a: Groups multiple FHIR resources into one container
c: Groups multiple FHIR resources into one container
Encrypts individual resources for secure transport
Validates resource schemas against FHIR profiles
Converts HL7 v2 messages into FHIR format data
informatics.e: A FHIR Bundle is a container resource that holds a collection of resources, used for transaction processing, search results, document bundles, and message passing.

informatics.q: Which HL7 FHIR resource type would represent a radiation therapy treatment plan?
informatics.a: ServiceRequest or CarePlan resource type
c: Observation or DiagnosticReport resource
ServiceRequest or CarePlan resource type
Patient or Practitioner resource type only
Medication or MedicationRequest resource
informatics.e: Radiation therapy plans map to ServiceRequest or CarePlan FHIR resources. The CodeX RT FHIR implementation guide defines specific profiles for radiation therapy data exchange.

informatics.q: What is the primary goal of the TG-263 nomenclature standard?
informatics.a: Standardize structure naming in radiation therapy
c: Standardize structure naming in radiation therapy
Define dose calculation algorithm requirements only
Specify linac mechanical tolerance specifications
Establish patient positioning protocol standards
informatics.e: TG-263 provides standardized nomenclature for anatomic structures, target volumes, and organs at risk to improve data sharing, analysis, and automated plan checking.

informatics.q: According to TG-263, what is the standardized name for the left parotid gland?
informatics.a: Parotid_L as the standard TG-263 name
c: Parotid_L as the standard TG-263 name
Lt_Parotid as standard naming convention
L_PAROTID as uppercase naming standard
LeftParotidGland as camelCase standard
informatics.e: TG-263 uses the format OrganName_L or OrganName_R for lateralized structures. Parotid_L is the standard name for the left parotid gland.

informatics.q: In TG-263 nomenclature, how are target volumes named for a primary tumor?
informatics.a: GTV_Primary or GTV_p naming convention
c: GTV_Primary or GTV_p naming convention
Target_Gross_Volume naming convention
PrimaryGrossTarget naming as standard
GROSS_TV_PRIMARY standard convention
informatics.e: TG-263 uses GTV, CTV, PTV prefixes followed by descriptors. GTV_p or GTV_Primary designates the gross tumor volume of the primary site.

informatics.q: What naming convention does TG-263 recommend for planning organ-at-risk volumes?
informatics.a: PRV_OrganName with PRV prefix notation
c: PRV_OrganName with PRV prefix notation
OrganName_PRV with PRV suffix notation
PlanningRiskVolume_Organ naming format
OAR_PRV_OrganName combined prefix format
informatics.e: TG-263 recommends PRV as a prefix (e.g., PRV_SpinalCord) to denote planning organ-at-risk volumes that include a margin around the OAR.

informatics.q: What is ARIA in the context of radiation oncology information systems?
informatics.a: Varian's oncology information system (OIS)
c: Varian's oncology information system (OIS)
Elekta's treatment planning system software
An open-source dose calculation engine tool
A DICOM conformance testing utility program
informatics.e: ARIA is Varian Medical Systems' comprehensive oncology information system that manages patient records, treatment scheduling, plan data, and machine interface.

informatics.q: What is MOSAIQ in radiation oncology?
informatics.a: Elekta's oncology information system (OIS)
c: Elekta's oncology information system (OIS)
Varian's image-guided radiation therapy tool
A Monte Carlo dose calculation engine system
An open-source treatment planning platform
informatics.e: MOSAIQ (by Elekta, formerly IMPAC Medical Systems) is an oncology information system used for scheduling, electronic charting, treatment delivery management, and record/verify functions.

informatics.q: What is the primary function of a record and verify (R&V) system in radiation therapy?
informatics.a: Verify treatment parameters match the approved plan
c: Verify treatment parameters match the approved plan
Calculate radiation dose distributions for patients
Generate contours for organs at risk structures
Perform quality assurance tests on linear accelerators
informatics.e: R&V systems compare machine parameters (gantry angle, field size, MLC, MU) against the approved treatment plan before allowing beam delivery, preventing treatment errors.

informatics.q: Which parameter is NOT typically verified by a record and verify system before treatment?
informatics.a: Patient's blood pressure at time of treatment
c: Patient's blood pressure at time of treatment
Gantry angle and collimator rotation settings
MLC leaf positions for each treatment field
Monitor units prescribed for beam delivery
informatics.e: R&V systems verify machine parameters like gantry angle, collimator, MLC positions, energy, and MU. Patient vitals like blood pressure are clinical assessments outside R&V scope.

informatics.q: What tolerance does a typical R&V system use for gantry angle verification?
informatics.a: Within 1 to 2 degrees of planned value
c: Within 1 to 2 degrees of planned value
Within 5 to 10 degrees of planned value
Within 0.01 degrees of the planned value
Exact integer match with no tolerance range
informatics.e: R&V systems typically use gantry angle tolerances of 1-2 degrees. Tighter tolerances may be applied for stereotactic treatments.

informatics.q: What network architecture is most commonly used in radiation oncology departments?
informatics.a: Client-server with dedicated VLAN segmentation
c: Peer-to-peer with direct device connections
Client-server with dedicated VLAN segmentation
Standalone isolated systems with no networking
Mesh network with decentralized node topology
informatics.e: Radiation oncology departments typically use client-server architecture with VLAN segmentation to isolate medical devices from general hospital traffic for security and performance.

informatics.q: What is a VLAN and why is it important in radiation oncology networks?
informatics.a: Virtual LAN that logically segments network traffic
c: Virtual LAN that logically segments network traffic
Virus and Logging Analysis Network security tool
Variable Length Address Notation for IP routing
Validated Local Area Node for device management
informatics.e: A VLAN (Virtual Local Area Network) logically segments a physical network, isolating RT equipment traffic from hospital network to improve security, performance, and manageability.

informatics.q: Which network protocol is used for DICOM communication between medical devices?
informatics.a: TCP/IP (Transmission Control Protocol/IP)
c: TCP/IP (Transmission Control Protocol/IP)
UDP (User Datagram Protocol) exclusively
FTP (File Transfer Protocol) for all data
SMTP (Simple Mail Transfer Protocol) only
informatics.e: DICOM uses TCP/IP for reliable, connection-oriented communication between Application Entities. TCP ensures data integrity through error checking and retransmission.

informatics.q: What is the purpose of a VPN in a radiation oncology network?
informatics.a: Creates encrypted tunnel for secure remote access
c: Creates encrypted tunnel for secure remote access
Increases network bandwidth for image transfers
Replaces all firewalls in the network perimeter
Provides wireless access points for mobile users
informatics.e: A VPN (Virtual Private Network) creates an encrypted tunnel over public networks, enabling secure remote access to treatment planning systems and patient data.

informatics.q: Which HIPAA rule specifically addresses electronic protected health information security?
informatics.a: The HIPAA Security Rule for ePHI safeguards
c: The HIPAA Security Rule for ePHI safeguards
The HIPAA Privacy Rule for data use controls
The HIPAA Breach Notification Rule provisions
The HIPAA Enforcement Rule penalty guidelines
informatics.e: The HIPAA Security Rule establishes administrative, physical, and technical safeguards to protect ePHI. It applies to all electronic systems handling patient data including RT information systems.

informatics.q: What does the FDA recommend regarding cybersecurity for medical devices?
informatics.a: Premarket and postmarket cybersecurity risk management
c: Premarket and postmarket cybersecurity risk management
Only premarket testing with no ongoing monitoring
Annual cybersecurity audits without design controls
Voluntary self-certification with no FDA oversight
informatics.e: FDA guidance requires manufacturers to address cybersecurity throughout the total product lifecycle, including premarket design controls and postmarket surveillance and patching.

informatics.q: What is the minimum recommended approach for authenticating users on radiation therapy systems?
informatics.a: Unique user IDs with strong password policies
c: Unique user IDs with strong password policies
Shared departmental login credentials for all
No authentication required for clinical systems
Physical key locks only without digital access
informatics.e: HIPAA and FDA guidance require unique user identification with strong passwords. Role-based access control and audit trails should complement authentication.

informatics.q: What is the primary concern with using legacy operating systems on medical linear accelerators?
informatics.a: Unpatched security vulnerabilities and exploits
c: Unpatched security vulnerabilities and exploits
Faster processing speed than modern systems
Better compatibility with current DICOM systems
Reduced storage requirements for patient data
informatics.e: Legacy operating systems (e.g., Windows XP, Windows 7) no longer receive security updates, leaving medical devices vulnerable to malware, ransomware, and network attacks.

informatics.q: What type of cybersecurity attack encrypts hospital data and demands payment for release?
informatics.a: Ransomware attack targeting healthcare data
c: Ransomware attack targeting healthcare data
Phishing attack for credential harvesting
Denial of service flooding the network
Man-in-the-middle intercepting data flow
informatics.e: Ransomware encrypts files and demands payment for decryption keys. Healthcare is frequently targeted, and such attacks can halt radiation treatments and endanger patients.

informatics.q: What is data integrity in the context of radiation therapy information systems?
informatics.a: Assurance that data is accurate and unaltered
c: Assurance that data is accurate and unaltered
Encryption of all data during network transfer
Compression of data for efficient storage use
Redundant backup copies on multiple servers
informatics.e: Data integrity ensures that treatment parameters, dose data, and patient information remain accurate, complete, and unchanged throughout their lifecycle in RT systems.

informatics.q: What validation check should be performed after DICOM transfer of an RT plan?
informatics.a: Compare checksum and key plan parameters at both ends
c: Compare checksum and key plan parameters at both ends
Only verify that the file transfer completed in time
Check the file size alone without parameter review
No validation needed if the network is reliable
informatics.e: After DICOM transfer, critical parameters (MU, gantry angles, MLC positions, patient ID) should be compared between TPS and R&V system to ensure data integrity.

informatics.q: What is a common method for validating data integrity in database systems?
informatics.a: Checksums and hash functions such as MD5 or SHA
c: Checksums and hash functions such as MD5 or SHA
Visual inspection of every record by staff only
Random sampling of less than one percent of data
Relying solely on RAID hardware error correction
informatics.e: Cryptographic hash functions (MD5, SHA-256) generate fixed-size digests that detect any modification to data. They are used to verify file and database integrity.

informatics.q: What is the purpose of independent MU verification in treatment plan transfer?
informatics.a: Confirm monitor units using a separate calculation
c: Confirm monitor units using a separate calculation
Verify that the treatment machine is calibrated
Check patient identity before treatment delivery
Validate the CT simulation image data quality
informatics.e: Independent MU verification uses a second calculation method to confirm the TPS-computed monitor units, catching potential errors in dose calculation or plan transfer.

informatics.q: What is an IHE integration profile in healthcare IT?
informatics.a: Specifies how standards solve a clinical workflow
c: Specifies how standards solve a clinical workflow
Defines new proprietary communication protocols
Replaces DICOM with a simpler data format type
Creates vendor-specific device driver software
informatics.e: IHE (Integrating the Healthcare Enterprise) profiles specify how existing standards (DICOM, HL7) should be applied to address specific clinical integration needs and workflows.

informatics.q: What does IHE-RO stand for and what is its focus?
informatics.a: IHE Radiation Oncology domain integration profiles
c: IHE Radiation Oncology domain integration profiles
International Health Exchange for Radiology Only
Integrated Hospital Electronics for Remote Ops
Information Highway Exchange for Research Orgs
informatics.e: IHE-RO develops integration profiles specific to radiation oncology workflows, addressing treatment planning, delivery, and information management interoperability challenges.

informatics.q: What is a PACS system used for in a radiation oncology department?
informatics.a: Storing, retrieving, and displaying medical images
c: Storing, retrieving, and displaying medical images
Calculating radiation dose for treatment planning
Scheduling patient appointments and treatments
Controlling the linear accelerator beam delivery
informatics.e: PACS (Picture Archiving and Communication System) manages medical images including CT, MRI, and PET scans used for treatment planning in radiation oncology.

informatics.q: What are the four main components of a PACS system?
informatics.a: Imaging modalities, network, archive, and workstations
c: Imaging modalities, network, archive, and workstations
CPU, RAM, hard drive, and network interface cards
Scanner, printer, fax machine, and display monitor
Database, web server, firewall, and backup drives
informatics.e: A PACS consists of imaging modalities (CT, MR), a secure network for transmission, an archive server for storage, and display workstations for viewing and interpretation.

informatics.q: What storage technology is commonly used for long-term PACS image archiving?
informatics.a: Network Attached Storage (NAS) or cloud storage
c: Network Attached Storage (NAS) or cloud storage
Local USB flash drives at each workstation
Optical disc jukebox systems exclusively used
Volatile RAM-based storage for fast access only
informatics.e: Modern PACS archives use NAS, SAN, or cloud storage solutions for scalable, redundant long-term storage. Legacy systems used optical disc jukeboxes.

informatics.q: What is the recommended frequency for full database backups of an oncology information system?
informatics.a: Daily full backups with transaction log backups
c: Daily full backups with transaction log backups
Monthly full backups without incremental backups
Annual backup at the end of each fiscal year
No regular schedule with only on-demand backups
informatics.e: Best practice recommends daily full backups supplemented by frequent transaction log backups (every 15-30 minutes) to minimize data loss in case of system failure.

informatics.q: What is an RPO (Recovery Point Objective) in disaster recovery planning?
informatics.a: Maximum acceptable data loss measured in time units
c: Maximum acceptable data loss measured in time units
The total cost of recovering from a disaster event
Number of staff required for recovery operations
Physical distance between primary and backup sites
informatics.e: RPO defines the maximum tolerable period of data loss. For RT systems, a low RPO (minutes) means frequent backups are needed to preserve treatment records.

informatics.q: What is an RTO (Recovery Time Objective) in disaster recovery?
informatics.a: Maximum acceptable downtime after a system failure
c: Maximum acceptable downtime after a system failure
Required time to complete a full system backup job
Time needed to train staff on the recovery process
Duration of the annual disaster recovery test drill
informatics.e: RTO is the target time to restore systems after a failure. For RT departments, short RTOs are critical to avoid treatment interruptions that affect patient outcomes.

informatics.q: What RAID level provides mirroring for redundancy without striping?
informatics.a: RAID 1 disk mirroring for data redundancy
c: RAID 0 striping for performance improvement
RAID 1 disk mirroring for data redundancy
RAID 5 striping with distributed parity data
RAID 6 striping with double parity protection
informatics.e: RAID 1 mirrors data across two or more disks, providing full redundancy. If one disk fails, the other contains an identical copy of all data.

informatics.q: What is the purpose of integrating an EHR with a radiation oncology information system?
informatics.a: Share patient demographics and clinical data seamlessly
c: Share patient demographics and clinical data seamlessly
Replace the treatment planning system functionality
Eliminate the need for DICOM communication protocols
Provide direct control of the linear accelerator beam
informatics.e: EHR-OIS integration enables sharing of patient demographics, allergies, lab results, clinical notes, and treatment summaries between radiation oncology and other departments.

informatics.q: Which standard is commonly used to integrate EHR systems with radiation oncology OIS?
informatics.a: HL7 messages (ADT, ORM, ORU) or FHIR APIs
c: HL7 messages (ADT, ORM, ORU) or FHIR APIs
DICOM only without any HL7 communication
Proprietary vendor-specific protocols exclusively
Manual data re-entry without automated exchange
informatics.e: HL7 v2 messages (ADT for demographics, ORM for orders) and increasingly FHIR APIs are used for bidirectional EHR-OIS integration in radiation oncology.

informatics.q: What is a machine log file in the context of linear accelerator treatments?
informatics.a: Record of actual machine parameters during delivery
c: Record of actual machine parameters during delivery
Maintenance schedule log for preventive service
Patient demographic data stored on the machine
Software installation and update history records
informatics.e: Machine log files (e.g., Varian Dynalog/Trajectory logs, Elekta iCom logs) record actual gantry, MLC, dose rate, and MU data during treatment delivery at high temporal resolution.

informatics.q: What is the typical temporal resolution of Varian trajectory log files?
informatics.a: Every 20 milliseconds during beam delivery
c: Every 20 milliseconds during beam delivery
Once per second during beam-on treatment
Every 500 milliseconds during delivery time
Once per control point in the treatment plan
informatics.e: Varian trajectory log files record machine parameters every 20 ms, providing high temporal resolution data for analyzing MLC positions, gantry angle, and dose rate during VMAT delivery.

informatics.q: What is the primary use of machine log file analysis in quality assurance?
informatics.a: Verify actual delivery matches planned parameters
c: Verify actual delivery matches planned parameters
Calculate the initial treatment plan dose values
Generate patient scheduling and billing reports
Create machine maintenance work order requests
informatics.e: Log file analysis compares actual delivered parameters (MLC positions, gantry angles, MU) against planned values to verify delivery accuracy and identify systematic errors.

informatics.q: What information is recorded in Varian Dynalog files?
informatics.a: Planned and actual MLC positions per leaf pair
c: Planned and actual MLC positions per leaf pair
Patient vital signs during treatment sessions
Dose volume histogram data for all structures
CT image pixel data from simulation scans
informatics.e: Dynalog files record planned vs actual MLC leaf positions, gantry angle, collimator angle, and other machine parameters every 50 ms for each treatment field.

informatics.q: What is ESAPI in the context of radiation therapy automation?
informatics.a: Eclipse Scripting Application Programming Interface
c: Eclipse Scripting Application Programming Interface
Electronic Safety and Protection Interface system
External System Access Point for Integration
Elekta Scripting and Automation Platform Inc
informatics.e: ESAPI is Varian's scripting API for Eclipse TPS, allowing medical physicists to automate plan checks, extract data, perform calculations, and create clinical tools using C#.

informatics.q: What programming language is used for Varian ESAPI scripts?
informatics.a: C# (C-Sharp) in the .NET framework
c: C# (C-Sharp) in the .NET framework
Python with specialized medical libraries
Java with the Eclipse IDE environment
MATLAB with medical physics toolboxes
informatics.e: ESAPI scripts are written in C# using the .NET framework. They can be run as standalone executables or within the Eclipse Script Wizard environment.

informatics.q: What Python library is commonly used for reading DICOM files in medical physics?
informatics.a: pydicom library for DICOM file parsing
c: pydicom library for DICOM file parsing
NumPy library for numerical array computing
pandas library for data frame manipulation
scikit-learn library for machine learning tasks
informatics.e: pydicom is the standard Python library for reading, modifying, and writing DICOM files. It provides access to all DICOM data elements and pixel data.

informatics.q: What is a common use of Python scripting in radiation therapy physics?
informatics.a: Automated plan quality checks and data analysis
c: Automated plan quality checks and data analysis
Direct control of linear accelerator beam output
Manufacturing of radiation therapy equipment parts
Patient scheduling and insurance claim processing
informatics.e: Python scripts are widely used for automated plan checking, log file analysis, QA data trending, dose calculation verification, and treatment outcome analysis.

informatics.q: What can ESAPI scripts access in the Eclipse treatment planning system?
informatics.a: Patient data, plan parameters, and dose distributions
c: Patient data, plan parameters, and dose distributions
Linear accelerator hardware control interfaces
Hospital billing and insurance claim systems
MRI scanner acquisition protocol parameters
informatics.e: ESAPI provides read access to patient demographics, structure sets, beam parameters, dose grids, DVH data, and plan optimization results within Eclipse.

informatics.q: What is the difference between a 'plugin' and a 'standalone' ESAPI script?
informatics.a: Plugin runs inside Eclipse; standalone runs externally
c: Plugin runs inside Eclipse; standalone runs externally
Plugin is read-only; standalone has write capability
Plugin uses Python; standalone requires C# language
Plugin is compiled code; standalone is interpreted
informatics.e: ESAPI plugins execute within the Eclipse application context, while standalone applications run as separate executables that connect to the Eclipse database externally.

informatics.q: What is cloud computing in the context of radiation therapy?
informatics.a: Remote computing resources accessed over the internet
c: Remote computing resources accessed over the internet
Local server room computing within the hospital
Portable laptop-based treatment planning stations
Dedicated hardware appliances at each treatment unit
informatics.e: Cloud computing provides on-demand access to shared computing resources (servers, storage, applications) via the internet, enabling scalable TPS, AI, and data analytics solutions.

informatics.q: What is a major concern with using public cloud services for radiation therapy patient data?
informatics.a: HIPAA compliance and data privacy requirements
c: HIPAA compliance and data privacy requirements
Cloud services are always too slow for imaging
Cloud computing cannot handle DICOM file formats
Public clouds lack sufficient storage capacity now
informatics.e: Storing PHI in the cloud requires HIPAA-compliant infrastructure including encryption, access controls, Business Associate Agreements, and data residency considerations.

informatics.q: Which cloud service model provides infrastructure like virtual machines and storage?
informatics.a: IaaS (Infrastructure as a Service) cloud model
c: IaaS (Infrastructure as a Service) cloud model
SaaS (Software as a Service) cloud delivery
PaaS (Platform as a Service) cloud framework
DaaS (Desktop as a Service) cloud computing
informatics.e: IaaS provides virtualized computing resources (VMs, storage, networking) on demand. Users manage the OS and applications while the provider manages the hardware infrastructure.

informatics.q: What type of cloud deployment keeps all data within the organization's own infrastructure?
informatics.a: Private cloud deployment within the institution
c: Private cloud deployment within the institution
Public cloud shared multi-tenant environment
Hybrid cloud combining public and private use
Community cloud shared among similar entities
informatics.e: A private cloud is operated solely for one organization, offering greater control over data, security, and compliance while still providing cloud computing benefits.

informatics.q: What is artificial intelligence (AI) primarily used for in radiation therapy?
informatics.a: Auto-segmentation, planning, and outcome prediction
c: Auto-segmentation, planning, and outcome prediction
Replacing all physicists in clinical departments
Manufacturing of linear accelerator components
Managing hospital cafeteria food ordering systems
informatics.e: AI in RT is used for auto-contouring, treatment planning optimization, quality assurance, outcome prediction, adaptive therapy, and image analysis tasks.

informatics.q: What type of machine learning architecture is most commonly used for auto-segmentation?
informatics.a: Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) such as U-Net
c: Convolutional Neural Networks (CNN) such as U-Net
Simple linear regression statistical models only
Decision tree random forest ensemble classifiers
Support vector machines with radial basis kernel
informatics.e: CNNs, particularly the U-Net architecture, are the dominant approach for medical image segmentation due to their ability to learn spatial features at multiple scales.

informatics.q: What is the U-Net architecture in deep learning for medical imaging?
informatics.a: Encoder-decoder network with skip connections design
c: Encoder-decoder network with skip connections design
Fully connected network without convolutional layers
Recurrent network for sequential data processing
Generative adversarial network for image synthesis
informatics.e: U-Net features a contracting encoder path and an expanding decoder path with skip connections that preserve spatial information, making it ideal for medical image segmentation.

informatics.q: What is the Dice similarity coefficient used to evaluate in auto-segmentation?
informatics.a: Overlap between predicted and reference contours
c: Overlap between predicted and reference contours
Speed of the segmentation algorithm processing
Number of training images used for the model
Resolution of the input CT image pixel spacing
informatics.e: The Dice coefficient (DSC) measures spatial overlap between two segmentations, ranging from 0 (no overlap) to 1 (perfect overlap). It is the primary metric for auto-segmentation evaluation.

informatics.q: What is the Hausdorff distance metric in auto-segmentation evaluation?
informatics.a: Maximum distance between two contour surfaces
c: Maximum distance between two contour surfaces
Average distance between contour center points
Volume ratio between two segmented structures
Number of voxels inside the segmented contour
informatics.e: The Hausdorff distance measures the maximum of the minimum distances between points on two contour surfaces, identifying the worst-case segmentation disagreement.

informatics.q: What is transfer learning in the context of deep learning for radiation therapy?
informatics.a: Using a pre-trained model and fine-tuning on new data
c: Using a pre-trained model and fine-tuning on new data
Transferring patient data between hospital databases
Moving treatment plans from one TPS to another system
Copying network weights without any model retraining
informatics.e: Transfer learning leverages knowledge from a model trained on a large dataset (e.g., ImageNet) and adapts it to a specific medical imaging task with limited training data.

informatics.q: What is knowledge-based planning (KBP) in radiation therapy?
informatics.a: Uses prior plan data to predict achievable dose goals
c: Uses prior plan data to predict achievable dose goals
Plans based solely on textbook dose prescriptions
Manual planning without any computer optimization
Planning using only forward dose calculations method
informatics.e: KBP systems like RapidPlan use a library of high-quality prior plans to build models that predict achievable DVH objectives for new patients based on their anatomy.

informatics.q: What is Varian RapidPlan used for in treatment planning?
informatics.a: DVH estimation from a trained model of prior plans
c: DVH estimation from a trained model of prior plans
Automatic CT image reconstruction from raw data
Direct dose calculation using Monte Carlo methods
Patient scheduling and treatment room assignment
informatics.e: RapidPlan uses a statistical model trained on a library of clinically approved plans to estimate achievable DVHs for new patients, guiding the optimization process.

informatics.q: What is the minimum recommended number of plans to train a RapidPlan model?
informatics.a: Approximately 20 to 40 high-quality treatment plans
c: Approximately 20 to 40 high-quality treatment plans
A minimum of 500 plans for any clinical model
Only 2 to 3 example plans are needed to train
Exactly 100 plans required with no flexibility
informatics.e: Varian recommends approximately 20-40 high-quality, clinically representative plans for training a RapidPlan model, though more plans generally improve model robustness.

informatics.q: What geometric feature does RapidPlan use to predict OAR dose?
informatics.a: Overlap volume histogram (OVH) with the PTV target
c: Overlap volume histogram (OVH) with the PTV target
CT Hounsfield unit histogram of the OAR region
MLC leaf sequence complexity scoring of the plan
Patient body mass index and body habitus data
informatics.e: RapidPlan uses the overlap volume histogram (OVH), which characterizes the geometric relationship between each OAR and the PTV, as a key predictor of achievable dose.

informatics.q: What is a digital signature used for in radiation therapy treatment records?
informatics.a: Authenticate identity and ensure document integrity
c: Authenticate identity and ensure document integrity
Encrypt all treatment data for network transfer
Compress treatment plan files to reduce file size
Generate random passwords for system user accounts
informatics.e: Digital signatures verify the signer's identity and ensure that signed treatment documents (plans, prescriptions) have not been altered, supporting regulatory compliance.

informatics.q: What is an audit trail in a radiation therapy information system?
informatics.a: Chronological record of all system access and changes
c: Chronological record of all system access and changes
Physical walkthrough inspection of the facility
Annual financial audit of department expenditures
Patient satisfaction survey results and reports
informatics.e: Audit trails log who accessed or modified data, when, and what changes were made. They are required by HIPAA and essential for investigating treatment incidents.

informatics.q: Which standard governs the software development lifecycle for medical device software?
informatics.a: IEC 62304 Medical Device Software Lifecycle
c: IEC 62304 Medical Device Software Lifecycle
ISO 9001 General Quality Management Systems
IEEE 802.11 Wireless Network Standards only
IEC 61217 Coordinates and Movements standard
informatics.e: IEC 62304 defines the lifecycle requirements for medical device software development including requirements, design, implementation, testing, maintenance, and risk management.

informatics.q: What are the three software safety classes defined in IEC 62304?
informatics.a: Class A (no injury), B (non-serious), C (serious)
c: Class A (no injury), B (non-serious), C (serious)
Level 1 (low), Level 2 (medium), Level 3 (high)
Grade I (minor), Grade II (major), Grade III (critical)
Category alpha, beta, and gamma risk designations
informatics.e: IEC 62304 classifies software into Class A (no injury possible), Class B (non-serious injury possible), and Class C (death or serious injury possible), each with increasing rigor requirements.

informatics.q: What IEC 62304 class would treatment planning system software typically be assigned?
informatics.a: Class C (death or serious injury possible)
c: Class A (no contribution to hazard possible)
Class B (non-serious injury is possible risk)
Class C (death or serious injury possible)
Not classified under IEC 62304 standards
informatics.e: TPS software directly affects patient treatment and dose delivery. Errors could cause death or serious injury, placing it in the highest safety class (Class C) under IEC 62304.

informatics.q: What is software verification versus validation in medical device development?
informatics.a: Verification confirms design specs; validation confirms user needs
c: Verification confirms design specs; validation confirms user needs
Verification tests hardware; validation tests software modules
Verification is done by users; validation by developers only
Verification is optional; validation is legally mandated only
informatics.e: Verification asks 'Did we build the product right?' (meets specifications), while validation asks 'Did we build the right product?' (meets user needs and intended use).

informatics.q: What is the TCP in TCP/IP networking?
informatics.a: Transmission Control Protocol for reliable data delivery
c: Transmission Control Protocol for reliable data delivery
Treatment Control Program for machine operation
Total Communication Platform for device linking
Temporal Compression Protocol for data reduction
informatics.e: TCP provides reliable, ordered, and error-checked delivery of data between applications. It establishes connections and ensures all packets are received correctly.

informatics.q: What is the difference between TCP and UDP network protocols?
informatics.a: TCP is reliable and connection-oriented; UDP is not
c: TCP is reliable and connection-oriented; UDP is not
UDP is more reliable and slower than TCP always
TCP is used only for email; UDP for web browsing
There is no practical difference between the two
informatics.e: TCP provides reliable, ordered delivery with error checking and retransmission. UDP is connectionless and faster but does not guarantee delivery, order, or integrity.

informatics.q: What subnet mask represents a /24 network in IPv4 addressing?
informatics.a: 255.255.255.0 providing 254 usable host addresses
c: 255.255.255.0 providing 254 usable host addresses
255.255.0.0 providing 65534 usable host addresses
255.0.0.0 providing millions of usable addresses
255.255.255.255 providing single host only address
informatics.e: A /24 subnet mask (255.255.255.0) allocates 8 bits for host addresses, providing 254 usable addresses (256 minus network and broadcast addresses).

informatics.q: What is the purpose of a firewall in a radiation oncology network?
informatics.a: Filter network traffic based on security rules applied
c: Filter network traffic based on security rules applied
Increase network speed by compressing all data flows
Store patient images as a backup archive server
Provide wireless access to mobile devices in clinic
informatics.e: Firewalls monitor and control incoming/outgoing network traffic based on predetermined security rules, protecting RT systems from unauthorized access and cyber threats.

informatics.q: What DICOM service is used to search for patients or studies on a PACS?
informatics.a: C-FIND query service for searching DICOM data
c: C-FIND query service for searching DICOM data
C-STORE service for pushing data to archives
C-ECHO service for verifying DICOM connectivity
N-CREATE service for creating new managed objects
informatics.e: C-FIND is the DICOM query service that searches for patients, studies, series, or instances matching specified criteria on a Query/Retrieve SCP (like a PACS).

informatics.q: What is DICOM-RT and how does it differ from standard DICOM?
informatics.a: DICOM extensions for radiation therapy specific objects
c: DICOM extensions for radiation therapy specific objects
A completely separate standard unrelated to DICOM
DICOM real-time streaming for live video feeds
A deprecated version of the original DICOM format
informatics.e: DICOM-RT extends DICOM with Information Object Definitions specific to radiation therapy: RT Plan, RT Dose, RT Structure Set, RT Image, and RT Treatment Record.

informatics.q: What is a DICOM Application Entity Title (AET)?
informatics.a: Unique identifier for a DICOM node on the network
c: Unique identifier for a DICOM node on the network
Encryption key used for secure DICOM data transfer
Patient name field within the DICOM file header
Software version number of the DICOM application
informatics.e: An AE Title is a string that uniquely identifies a DICOM application entity on the network. It is used during association negotiation to identify source and destination.

informatics.q: What is the DICOM Modality Worklist (MWL) service used for?
informatics.a: Provides scheduled procedure information to modalities
c: Provides scheduled procedure information to modalities
Stores completed examination reports in the archive
Calculates radiation dose from treatment plan data
Manages user authentication on imaging workstations
informatics.e: Modality Worklist provides scheduled patient and procedure information from the RIS/OIS to imaging modalities, reducing manual data entry errors at the scanner.

informatics.q: What is the purpose of DICOM Structured Reporting (SR)?
informatics.a: Encodes clinical reports in structured machine-readable form
c: Encodes clinical reports in structured machine-readable form
Converts unstructured text to PDF image format only
Provides real-time voice dictation transcription service
Generates visual charts from numerical data tables
informatics.e: DICOM SR encodes clinical measurements, findings, and reports in a structured, coded format that can be stored, transmitted, queried, and processed by computer systems.

informatics.q: What is the DICOM Information Model hierarchy for query/retrieve?
informatics.a: Patient, Study, Series, Instance (composite level)
c: Patient, Study, Series, Instance (composite level)
Hospital, Department, Room, Equipment level only
Image, Pixel, Frame, Slice ordering hierarchy
Database, Table, Row, Column structure layout
informatics.e: The DICOM Query/Retrieve information model follows the hierarchy: Patient -> Study -> Series -> Instance, allowing queries at each level of specificity.

informatics.q: What is data de-identification in the context of medical imaging research?
informatics.a: Removing or obscuring patient identifying information
c: Removing or obscuring patient identifying information
Compressing image data to reduce storage file size
Converting images from one modality format to another
Enhancing image quality through advanced filtering
informatics.e: De-identification removes or modifies DICOM tags containing PHI (patient name, DOB, MRN) to enable data sharing for research while protecting patient privacy per HIPAA.

informatics.q: Which DICOM supplement specifically addresses de-identification profiles?
informatics.a: DICOM PS3.15 Annex E confidentiality profiles
c: DICOM PS3.15 Annex E confidentiality profiles
DICOM PS3.3 Information Object Definitions only
DICOM PS3.7 Message Exchange protocol section
DICOM PS3.10 Media Storage and File Format
informatics.e: DICOM PS3.15 Annex E defines confidentiality profiles specifying which attributes to remove, replace, or modify for de-identification at different levels of protection.

informatics.q: What is the Safe Harbor method of HIPAA de-identification?
informatics.a: Remove 18 specific identifiers from patient records
c: Remove 18 specific identifiers from patient records
Encrypt all data with 256-bit AES encryption keys
Obtain patient consent before sharing any data
Apply statistical analysis to quantify re-ID risk
informatics.e: The HIPAA Safe Harbor method requires removal of 18 specific identifiers (name, dates, zip codes, SSN, MRN, etc.) with no actual knowledge that remaining information could identify individuals.

informatics.q: What is the Expert Determination method of HIPAA de-identification?
informatics.a: Statistical expert certifies re-identification risk is small
c: Statistical expert certifies re-identification risk is small
IT department reviews and approves all data sharing
IRB committee votes on each data release request
All 18 Safe Harbor identifiers are removed from data
informatics.e: The Expert Determination method requires a qualified statistical expert to determine and certify that the risk of re-identifying an individual is very small.

informatics.q: What is a DICOM Conformance Statement?
informatics.a: Document describing a product's DICOM capabilities
c: Document describing a product's DICOM capabilities
Legal contract between vendor and hospital buyer
Patient consent form for imaging data collection
Software license agreement for DICOM viewer tools
informatics.e: A DICOM Conformance Statement details which SOP classes, transfer syntaxes, and services a product supports, essential for planning interoperability between systems.

informatics.q: What is the RT Beams Treatment Record IOD used for in DICOM-RT?
informatics.a: Records actual delivered treatment beam parameters
c: Records actual delivered treatment beam parameters
Stores planned beam geometry before delivery only
Contains the CT images used for beam simulation
Defines quality assurance test beam configurations
informatics.e: The RT Beams Treatment Record stores actual delivered treatment parameters including delivered MU, gantry angles, and MLC positions, providing a permanent treatment delivery record.

informatics.q: What is the purpose of the RT Ion Plan IOD in DICOM?
informatics.a: Stores treatment plan data for particle beam therapy
c: Stores treatment plan data for particle beam therapy
Contains ion chamber measurement calibration data
Records ionization readings from dosimetry devices
Defines ionic contrast agent injection protocols
informatics.e: The RT Ion Plan IOD extends the RT Plan for particle therapy (proton, carbon ion), including energy layers, spot positions, and scanning patterns specific to particle beams.

informatics.q: What is DICOMweb and how does it differ from traditional DICOM networking?
informatics.a: RESTful web services for DICOM using HTTP/HTTPS
c: RESTful web services for DICOM using HTTP/HTTPS
A web browser plugin for viewing DICOM images
A proprietary cloud storage service for images
An older version of the DICOM networking stack
informatics.e: DICOMweb provides RESTful services (WADO-RS, STOW-RS, QIDO-RS) over HTTP/HTTPS, enabling web-based and cloud DICOM interactions without traditional DIMSE protocols.

informatics.q: What does WADO stand for in DICOMweb services?
informatics.a: Web Access to DICOM Objects via HTTP protocol
c: Web Access to DICOM Objects via HTTP protocol
Wide Area Data Operations for network routing
Wireless Access to Distributed Online resources
Workstation Automated DICOM Output management
informatics.e: WADO (Web Access to DICOM Objects) allows retrieval of DICOM objects via standard HTTP requests, enabling web browsers and mobile apps to access medical images.

informatics.q: What is the role of HL7 ORM messages in radiation oncology workflow?
informatics.a: Communicate treatment orders between clinical systems
c: Communicate treatment orders between clinical systems
Transfer medical images between PACS and viewers
Monitor real-time vital signs of patients on table
Control linear accelerator beam-on beam-off states
informatics.e: ORM (Order) messages transmit order information (e.g., simulation orders, treatment orders) between the EHR, RIS, and OIS in radiation oncology workflows.

informatics.q: What is the HL7 ORU message type used for?
informatics.a: Transmitting unsolicited observation results and reports
c: Transmitting unsolicited observation results and reports
Ordering new radiology imaging examinations only
Registering new patients in the hospital system
Scheduling treatment appointments for all patients
informatics.e: ORU (Observation Result Unsolicited) messages transmit clinical observations, lab results, and reports from producing systems to requesting or interested systems.

informatics.q: In TG-263, what suffix is used to denote a right-sided structure?
informatics.a: The _R suffix (e.g., Lung_R, Kidney_R)
c: The _R suffix (e.g., Lung_R, Kidney_R)
The -Right suffix (e.g., Lung-Right format)
The _Rt suffix (e.g., Lung_Rt, Kidney_Rt)
The R_ prefix (e.g., R_Lung, R_Kidney)
informatics.e: TG-263 uses _L and _R suffixes for laterality designation, providing consistent naming across institutions (e.g., Parotid_R, Lung_R, Kidney_R).

informatics.q: What does TG-263 recommend for naming the spinal cord structure?
informatics.a: SpinalCord as the standardized structure name
c: SpinalCord as the standardized structure name
Spinal_Cord with an underscore separator
SC as an abbreviated short form name only
Cord_Spinal in reversed order naming style
informatics.e: TG-263 recommends SpinalCord (no space or underscore) as the standardized name. Consistent naming enables automated plan checking and multi-institutional data analysis.

informatics.q: What is the purpose of the TG-263 'TG263-Primary Name' field?
informatics.a: Provides the unique standardized name for each structure
c: Provides the unique standardized name for each structure
Stores the treating physician's name for that organ
Contains the dose constraint for each OAR structure
Records the contouring method used (auto or manual)
informatics.e: The TG263-Primary Name is the standardized, unique identifier for each anatomical structure, ensuring consistent naming across institutions and software platforms.

informatics.q: How does ARIA handle treatment plan approval workflow?
informatics.a: Multi-level electronic approval with role-based access
c: Multi-level electronic approval with role-based access
Single signature paper-based approval process only
Automatic approval without any human review step
Email-based approval chain outside the OIS system
informatics.e: ARIA supports configurable multi-level approval workflows where plans progress through planning, physics review, and physician approval with role-based access controls.

informatics.q: What is the function of the ARIA Treatment Management workspace?
informatics.a: Manage daily treatment delivery and session tracking
c: Manage daily treatment delivery and session tracking
Create new treatment plans from CT simulation data
Perform independent dose calculation verification
Generate machine QA reports and trending analysis
informatics.e: The Treatment Management workspace in ARIA provides the interface for managing daily treatment sessions, tracking delivered fractions, and interfacing with the treatment machine.

informatics.q: What database does MOSAIQ use for its backend data storage?
informatics.a: Microsoft SQL Server database management system
c: Microsoft SQL Server database management system
Oracle Database enterprise relational database
MySQL open-source community edition database
PostgreSQL open-source advanced SQL database
informatics.e: MOSAIQ, like ARIA, uses Microsoft SQL Server as its database backend for storing patient, treatment, and administrative data.

informatics.q: What is a key benefit of R&V system integration with the linear accelerator?
informatics.a: Prevents treatment delivery with incorrect parameters
c: Prevents treatment delivery with incorrect parameters
Increases the radiation output of the machine beam
Eliminates the need for daily machine quality checks
Automatically contours patient organs from CT images
informatics.e: R&V integration with the linac prevents beam-on unless machine parameters match the approved plan within tolerances, providing a critical safety interlock.

informatics.q: What is a tolerance table in a record and verify system?
informatics.a: Defines acceptable parameter deviation limits for delivery
c: Defines acceptable parameter deviation limits for delivery
Lists patient allergy information and medications
Contains the dose prescription for each treatment plan
Stores the machine calibration factors and data
informatics.e: Tolerance tables define the maximum acceptable deviation between planned and actual machine parameters (e.g., gantry angle +/- 2 degrees, MU +/- 1%) before the R&V system inhibits delivery.

informatics.q: What does the IHE-RO 'Treatment Delivery Record Content' profile specify?
informatics.a: Standard format for recording delivered treatment data
c: Standard format for recording delivered treatment data
Method for live streaming treatment delivery video
Protocol for verbal communication during treatment
Specification for room shielding design analysis
informatics.e: This profile standardizes how treatment delivery records are created and exchanged, ensuring consistent documentation of delivered treatment parameters across different vendor systems.

informatics.q: What is lossy compression in the context of medical image storage?
informatics.a: Compression that permanently discards some image data
c: Compression that permanently discards some image data
Compression that preserves all original image data
Encryption method that protects image data privacy
File format conversion without any data modification
informatics.e: Lossy compression reduces file size by permanently removing some data. It is generally not acceptable for primary diagnostic images but may be used for non-diagnostic review copies.

informatics.q: For radiation therapy planning CT images, is lossy compression generally acceptable?
informatics.a: No, lossless compression is required for RT planning
c: No, lossless compression is required for RT planning
Yes, any level of lossy compression is acceptable
Only JPEG lossy at 10:1 ratio is acceptable here
Compression is never used for any medical images
informatics.e: RT planning CT images are used for dose calculation where Hounsfield unit accuracy is critical. Lossy compression could alter CT numbers and affect dose accuracy.

informatics.q: What is the 3-2-1 backup rule for data protection?
informatics.a: 3 copies, 2 media types, 1 offsite backup location
c: 3 copies, 2 media types, 1 offsite backup location
3 daily backups, 2 weekly backups, 1 monthly cycle
3 servers, 2 switches, 1 firewall in the network
3 users review, 2 approve, 1 administrator signs off
informatics.e: The 3-2-1 rule recommends maintaining at least 3 copies of data on 2 different media types with 1 copy stored offsite for disaster recovery.

informatics.q: What is the purpose of a Business Continuity Plan (BCP) for a radiation oncology department?
informatics.a: Ensures continued patient treatment during disruptions
c: Ensures continued patient treatment during disruptions
Maximizes revenue and billing efficiency only today
Provides marketing strategy for patient recruitment
Defines annual budget allocation for equipment buys
informatics.e: A BCP outlines procedures to maintain critical operations (patient treatments) during system failures, natural disasters, or cyberattacks, including manual treatment delivery procedures.

informatics.q: What is the significance of the DICOM UID (Unique Identifier)?
informatics.a: Globally unique identifier for each DICOM object instance
c: Globally unique identifier for each DICOM object instance
User login ID for accessing the PACS workstation
Universal insurance identifier for billing purposes
Unit identification number for equipment inventory
informatics.e: DICOM UIDs ensure globally unique identification of each study, series, instance, and SOP class. They prevent conflicts when exchanging data between institutions.

informatics.q: What is the maximum length of a DICOM UID?
informatics.a: 64 characters composed of digits and periods
c: 64 characters composed of digits and periods
128 characters with alphanumeric characters only
256 characters with any printable ASCII values
32 characters using hexadecimal digit values
informatics.e: DICOM UIDs are limited to 64 characters consisting only of digits (0-9) and the period (.) character, following ISO 8824 object identifier value representation.

informatics.q: What is PHI in the context of HIPAA and healthcare data privacy?
informatics.a: Protected Health Information identifying a patient
c: Protected Health Information identifying a patient
Public Hospital Infrastructure and equipment data
Physician Hospital Identification number records
Pharmaceutical Health Index for drug tracking
informatics.e: PHI is individually identifiable health information held by covered entities, including demographics, diagnoses, treatment records, and billing data linked to a specific patient.

informatics.q: What is a DICOM tag and how is it structured?
informatics.a: Group and element number pair identifying a data element
c: Group and element number pair identifying a data element
Text label printed on the physical storage media disc
Barcode affixed to the patient identification bracelet
Color-coded label on network cables for organization
informatics.e: A DICOM tag is a pair of hexadecimal numbers (group, element) that uniquely identifies each data element, e.g., (0010,0010) for Patient Name, (0008,0060) for Modality.

informatics.q: What DICOM tag stores the patient's name?
informatics.a: (0010,0010) Patient Name tag identifier
c: (0010,0010) Patient Name tag identifier
(0008,0060) Modality tag for imaging type
(0020,000D) Study Instance UID tag value
(7FE0,0010) Pixel Data tag for image data
informatics.e: Tag (0010,0010) stores the Patient's Name in DICOM format (Last^First^Middle^Prefix^Suffix). It is one of the key tags that must be modified during de-identification.

informatics.q: What is role-based access control (RBAC) in radiation therapy information systems?
informatics.a: Assigns system permissions based on user job function
c: Assigns system permissions based on user job function
Grants all users identical full administrative access
Restricts access based on time of day constraints
Controls access using biometric fingerprint scans
informatics.e: RBAC assigns permissions based on roles (physicist, dosimetrist, therapist, physician) so users only access functions relevant to their responsibilities, supporting least privilege principles.

informatics.q: What is the purpose of network segmentation in a hospital with radiation therapy?
informatics.a: Isolate RT systems from other hospital network traffic
c: Isolate RT systems from other hospital network traffic
Increase internet browsing speed for clinical staff
Allow patients to access their treatment plan data
Reduce the cost of network hardware procurement
informatics.e: Network segmentation isolates radiation therapy devices on separate VLANs, limiting exposure to threats from the broader hospital network and controlling data flow.

informatics.q: What is the purpose of a DICOM Storage Commitment service?
informatics.a: Confirms that stored data is safely archived on server
c: Confirms that stored data is safely archived on server
Commits to purchasing additional storage disk space
Schedules automatic deletion of old study archives
Reserves storage quota for future incoming studies
informatics.e: Storage Commitment (N-ACTION/N-EVENT-REPORT) allows an SCU to request confirmation that data has been safely stored by the SCP, enabling the sender to delete its local copy.

informatics.q: What is the Explicit VR transfer syntax in DICOM?
informatics.a: Value representation is explicitly included in each element
c: Value representation is explicitly included in each element
Data is stored in big endian byte order exclusively
Pixel data is always compressed using JPEG format
Only numeric data types are supported in elements
informatics.e: In Explicit VR transfer syntax, each data element includes its Value Representation (data type), unlike Implicit VR where the VR must be looked up from the data dictionary.

informatics.q: What Python library is commonly used for dose analysis and gamma evaluation?
informatics.a: pymedphys library for medical physics analysis
c: pymedphys library for medical physics analysis
TensorFlow library for deep neural network tasks
Flask library for web application development use
Matplotlib library for generating chart graphics
informatics.e: pymedphys provides tools for gamma analysis, Mudensity calculation, log file analysis, and other medical physics tasks. Other options include DICOM-RT specific tools.

informatics.q: What is the primary purpose of ESAPI plan quality metric scripts?
informatics.a: Automate checking plan parameters against clinical goals
c: Automate checking plan parameters against clinical goals
Generate patient billing codes for treatment charges
Create marketing materials for department promotion
Schedule machine preventive maintenance appointments
informatics.e: ESAPI plan check scripts automatically evaluate plan quality metrics (hotspot, target coverage, OAR doses, field naming, prescription) against institutional protocols.

informatics.q: What is federated learning in the context of AI for radiation therapy?
informatics.a: Train AI models across institutions without sharing data
c: Train AI models across institutions without sharing data
Transfer trained model weights to a central repository
Combine all institutional data into one central store
Use only single-institution data for model training
informatics.e: Federated learning trains models collaboratively across multiple institutions by sharing model updates (gradients) rather than raw patient data, preserving privacy while improving model generalizability.

informatics.q: What is the Dice coefficient value that generally indicates clinically acceptable auto-segmentation?
informatics.a: Greater than 0.8 for most organs at risk structures
c: Greater than 0.8 for most organs at risk structures
Greater than 0.3 for all clinical applications
Exactly 1.0 required for any clinical acceptance
Less than 0.5 is considered adequate performance
informatics.e: A Dice coefficient >0.8 is generally considered clinically acceptable for most OARs, though requirements vary by structure (e.g., higher for large organs, lower accepted for small structures).

informatics.q: What is data augmentation in training deep learning models for medical imaging?
informatics.a: Artificially expanding training data via transformations
c: Artificially expanding training data via transformations
Collecting more patient data from clinical databases
Removing poor quality images from the training set
Increasing image resolution by interpolation methods
informatics.e: Data augmentation applies transformations (rotation, flipping, scaling, elastic deformation) to training images to increase dataset diversity and reduce overfitting in medical imaging AI.

informatics.q: What is model overfitting in machine learning for radiation therapy applications?
informatics.a: Model performs well on training data but poorly on new data
c: Model performs well on training data but poorly on new data
Model is too simple to capture any patterns in data
Model training takes too long to converge to solution
Model uses too little memory during training execution
informatics.e: Overfitting occurs when a model memorizes training data noise rather than learning general patterns, resulting in poor performance on unseen clinical data.

informatics.q: What is a validation dataset used for in machine learning model development?
informatics.a: Tune hyperparameters and monitor for overfitting issues
c: Tune hyperparameters and monitor for overfitting issues
Provide final unbiased assessment of model accuracy
Generate training examples for the learning process
Replace the test dataset in final model evaluation
informatics.e: The validation set is used during training to tune hyperparameters and detect overfitting. It is separate from the test set, which provides the final unbiased performance evaluation.

informatics.q: What is the difference between training, validation, and test datasets?
informatics.a: Train learns patterns; validation tunes; test gives final eval
c: Train learns patterns; validation tunes; test gives final eval
All three datasets serve the same identical purpose here
Training is largest; validation and test are not needed
Test data is used first, then training data afterward
informatics.e: Training data teaches the model, validation data tunes hyperparameters and monitors overfitting, and test data provides an unbiased final performance assessment.

informatics.q: What is RapidPlan's primary output that guides the optimization process?
informatics.a: Predicted DVH objectives for each OAR structure
c: Predicted DVH objectives for each OAR structure
Final optimized dose distribution ready for approval
Beam angle selection for optimal plan arrangement
Patient setup instructions for treatment delivery
informatics.e: RapidPlan generates predicted DVH curves for each OAR based on the patient's geometry, which are then used as optimization objectives in the Eclipse treatment planning system.

informatics.q: What is a principal component analysis (PCA) model used for in RapidPlan?
informatics.a: Reduces DVH dimensionality to extract key shape features
c: Reduces DVH dimensionality to extract key shape features
Calculates the principal radiation beam axis direction
Analyzes CT image principal components for quality
Determines the principal investigator for the study
informatics.e: RapidPlan uses PCA to reduce the high-dimensional DVH data into a smaller set of principal components, capturing the most important variations in dose distributions across the training plans.

informatics.q: What encryption standard is recommended for protecting ePHI at rest?
informatics.a: AES-256 (Advanced Encryption Standard 256-bit key)
c: AES-256 (Advanced Encryption Standard 256-bit key)
DES (Data Encryption Standard 56-bit key only)
ROT13 simple letter substitution cipher method
Base64 encoding for data format conversion only
informatics.e: AES-256 is the recommended encryption standard for protecting ePHI at rest. NIST and HIPAA guidance consider it sufficient for healthcare data protection.

informatics.q: What is TLS and why is it important for healthcare network communications?
informatics.a: Transport Layer Security encrypts data in transit
c: Transport Layer Security encrypts data in transit
Treatment Logging System records beam parameters
Therapeutic Laser Safety monitoring protocol used
Transmission Loss Standard for cable performance
informatics.e: TLS encrypts data during transmission over networks, preventing eavesdropping and tampering. It is essential for protecting PHI during DICOM, HL7, and web-based healthcare communications.

informatics.q: What is multi-factor authentication (MFA) in the context of medical system access?
informatics.a: Requires two or more verification methods to log in
c: Requires two or more verification methods to log in
Uses only a single strong password for all access
Allows anonymous access to non-clinical system data
Shares one credential among all department members
informatics.e: MFA combines something you know (password), something you have (token/phone), or something you are (biometric) to provide stronger authentication for accessing clinical systems.

informatics.q: What is a software Bill of Materials (SBOM) for medical devices?
informatics.a: List of all software components and dependencies used
c: List of all software components and dependencies used
Invoice listing hardware costs for the device unit
Regulatory approval documents from FDA submission
Patient instruction manual for the medical device
informatics.e: An SBOM catalogs all software components (including open-source libraries) in a medical device, enabling vulnerability tracking and cybersecurity risk management as recommended by FDA.

informatics.q: What is the FDA's approach to regulating AI/ML-based medical devices?
informatics.a: Total Product Lifecycle (TPLC) regulatory approach used
c: Total Product Lifecycle (TPLC) regulatory approach used
No regulation required for any AI-based algorithms
One-time premarket review with no ongoing oversight
Only post-market surveillance without premarket check
informatics.e: FDA uses a Total Product Lifecycle approach for AI/ML devices, addressing predetermined change control plans, good machine learning practices, and ongoing algorithm monitoring.

informatics.q: What is the purpose of a predetermined change control plan for AI medical devices?
informatics.a: Defines how algorithm updates will be validated and deployed
c: Defines how algorithm updates will be validated and deployed
Prevents any changes to the AI algorithm after approval
Requires complete re-submission for each minor update
Allows unlimited changes without any regulatory review
informatics.e: A predetermined change control plan, part of FDA's AI/ML framework, specifies anticipated modifications, validation methodology, and performance monitoring for algorithm updates.

informatics.q: What is a common metric for evaluating classification AI models in radiation therapy?
informatics.a: Area Under the ROC Curve (AUC) metric value
c: Area Under the ROC Curve (AUC) metric value
Lines of code in the model source code files
Training time measured in GPU-hours consumed
Number of layers in the neural network model
informatics.e: AUC measures the ability of a classification model to distinguish between classes across all thresholds. Values close to 1.0 indicate excellent discrimination performance.

informatics.q: What is the purpose of a GAN (Generative Adversarial Network) in radiation therapy?
informatics.a: Generate synthetic images or convert between modalities
c: Generate synthetic images or convert between modalities
Optimize treatment plan beam weights exclusively
Calculate dose distributions using Monte Carlo method
Perform patient scheduling and resource allocation
informatics.e: GANs in RT are used for synthetic CT generation from MRI (MR-only planning), data augmentation, image quality enhancement, and cross-modality image synthesis.

informatics.q: What is synthetic CT generation and why is it useful in radiation therapy?
informatics.a: Creates pseudo-CT from MRI for dose calculation planning
c: Creates pseudo-CT from MRI for dose calculation planning
Generates artificial patient anatomy for training only
Produces CT images without any radiation dose to body
Converts DICOM images to standard JPEG file format
informatics.e: Synthetic CT generation uses deep learning to create CT-equivalent images from MRI scans, enabling MR-only radiation therapy planning without additional CT simulation.

informatics.q: What is the DICOM tag (0008,0016) used to store?
informatics.a: SOP Class UID identifying the type of DICOM object
c: SOP Class UID identifying the type of DICOM object
Patient ID number for hospital medical records
Study date when the imaging exam was performed
Pixel data containing the actual image information
informatics.e: Tag (0008,0016) stores the SOP Class UID, which identifies the type of DICOM object (e.g., CT Image Storage, RT Plan Storage, RT Structure Set Storage).

informatics.q: What is the DICOM SOP Class UID for RT Plan Storage?
c: 1.2.840.10008.5.1.4.1.1.2 for CT Image Storage
informatics.e: The SOP Class UID 1.2.840.10008.5.1.4.1.1.481.5 identifies an RT Plan Storage object. Knowing SOP Class UIDs is important for DICOM configuration and troubleshooting.

informatics.q: What is the DICOM Grayscale Softcopy Presentation State used for?
informatics.a: Stores image display settings like window level and zoom
c: Stores image display settings like window level and zoom
Contains the grayscale pixel data of the CT image
Defines printer calibration for grayscale hard copies
Specifies the grayscale monitor QA testing protocol
informatics.e: Grayscale Softcopy Presentation State stores display parameters (window/level, zoom, annotations, overlays) separately from pixel data, enabling consistent image presentation.

informatics.q: What is DICOM MPPS (Modality Performed Procedure Step)?
informatics.a: Reports procedure status and details to information systems
c: Reports procedure status and details to information systems
Controls the modality hardware during image scanning
Encrypts images during transfer to the PACS archive
Schedules patient appointments on imaging modalities
informatics.e: MPPS allows modalities to report the status, timing, and details of performed procedures back to the RIS/OIS, enabling workflow tracking and billing information updates.

informatics.q: What is the purpose of DICOM Query/Retrieve at the Study Root level?
informatics.a: Search for studies matching criteria across all patients
c: Search for studies matching criteria across all patients
Retrieve only the most recent patient study created
Query individual pixel values within DICOM images
Search the root directory of the file system storage
informatics.e: Study Root Query/Retrieve allows searching for studies based on criteria like patient name, date, modality, and accession number across the entire PACS archive.

informatics.q: What is the significance of the DICOM tag (0020,000D)?
informatics.a: Study Instance UID uniquely identifying the imaging study
c: Study Instance UID uniquely identifying the imaging study
Series Instance UID identifying a series within study
SOP Instance UID identifying a single image instance
Frame of Reference UID for spatial coordinate system
informatics.e: Tag (0020,000D) contains the Study Instance UID, a globally unique identifier that groups all series and images belonging to a single imaging study or examination.

informatics.q: What is an HL7 interface engine used for in a hospital setting?
informatics.a: Routes and transforms messages between clinical systems
c: Routes and transforms messages between clinical systems
Provides direct patient care through automated means
Generates radiation dose from treatment plan input
Manufactures medical devices for hospital deployment
informatics.e: An interface engine (e.g., Mirth Connect, Rhapsody) routes HL7 messages between systems, performing message transformation, filtering, and protocol conversion as needed.

informatics.q: What is Mirth Connect used for in healthcare IT integration?
informatics.a: Open-source interface engine for HL7 message routing
c: Open-source interface engine for HL7 message routing
Commercial treatment planning system from Varian
Medical image viewer for radiology departments
Patient portal for accessing medical test results
informatics.e: Mirth Connect (NextGen Connect) is a widely used open-source interface engine that supports HL7 v2, FHIR, DICOM, and other healthcare standards for system integration.

informatics.q: What is the purpose of a FHIR Capability Statement?
informatics.a: Describes server's supported FHIR resources and operations
c: Describes server's supported FHIR resources and operations
States the financial capabilities of the organization
Lists the clinical capabilities of medical staff only
Defines hardware specifications of the server system
informatics.e: A FHIR Capability Statement (formerly Conformance) describes what resources, interactions, search parameters, and operations a FHIR server supports.

informatics.q: What is OAuth 2.0 used for in FHIR-based healthcare applications?
informatics.a: Authorization framework for secure API access control
c: Authorization framework for secure API access control
Data compression algorithm for reducing file sizes
Database query language for searching patient data
Image rendering engine for displaying medical scans
informatics.e: OAuth 2.0 provides delegated authorization for FHIR APIs, allowing applications to access protected health data on behalf of users without sharing credentials.

informatics.q: What is SMART on FHIR?
informatics.a: Framework for launching apps within EHR systems securely
c: Framework for launching apps within EHR systems securely
A type of machine learning algorithm for diagnosis
Hardware specification for medical device processors
Self-Monitoring Analysis and Reporting Technology
informatics.e: SMART on FHIR (Substitutable Medical Applications and Reusable Technologies) is a framework for building apps that can launch from any EHR using OAuth2 and OpenID Connect.

informatics.q: What is the CodeX RT FHIR implementation guide?
informatics.a: FHIR profiles for radiation therapy data exchange standard
c: FHIR profiles for radiation therapy data exchange standard
Coding standards for radiation therapy software apps
Hardware codex for radiation therapy machine design
Diagnostic code system for radiation therapy billing
informatics.e: The CodeX RT (Radiation Therapy) FHIR implementation guide defines standardized FHIR profiles for exchanging RT treatment summaries between oncology systems and EHRs.

informatics.q: What is a webhook and how might it be used in radiation therapy workflows?
informatics.a: HTTP callback that sends automated event notifications
c: HTTP callback that sends automated event notifications
Physical hook for mounting equipment on treatment couch
Web browser plugin for viewing treatment plan data
Security tool that hooks into malware detection scans
informatics.e: Webhooks send automated HTTP notifications when events occur (e.g., plan approved, QA completed), enabling real-time workflow automation in radiation therapy systems.

informatics.q: What is the purpose of TG-263 structure name mapping in auto-planning systems?
informatics.a: Enable automated template matching for plan optimization
c: Enable automated template matching for plan optimization
Create patient-specific anatomical illustrations only
Generate billing codes from structure set information
Track physician contouring time for efficiency data
informatics.e: Standardized TG-263 names allow auto-planning systems to automatically recognize structures and apply appropriate optimization objectives and dose constraints.

informatics.q: What does TG-263 recommend for naming the brainstem structure?
informatics.a: Brainstem as the standardized structure naming
c: Brainstem as the standardized structure naming
Brain_Stem with underscore separator format
BS as an abbreviated two-letter code name
Stem_Brain in reversed anatomical word order
informatics.e: TG-263 recommends Brainstem (single word, no underscore) as the standardized name, distinguishing it from Brain_Stem which some legacy systems may use.

informatics.q: What is the DICOM tag for Referenced Frame of Reference UID?
informatics.a: (0020,0052) Frame of Reference UID tag value
c: (0020,0052) Frame of Reference UID tag value
(0008,0018) SOP Instance UID tag identifying
(0010,0020) Patient ID tag for medical records
(0028,0010) Rows tag defining image dimensions
informatics.e: Tag (0020,0052) stores the Frame of Reference UID, which links images and RT objects that share the same spatial coordinate system for registration and fusion.

informatics.q: What is the purpose of database transaction logging in an OIS?
informatics.a: Records all database changes for recovery and auditing
c: Records all database changes for recovery and auditing
Logs only user login and logout session timestamps
Tracks network bandwidth usage for billing purposes
Monitors CPU temperature for hardware health status
informatics.e: Transaction logs record every database modification, enabling point-in-time recovery after failures and providing audit trails for treatment record changes.

informatics.q: What is hot standby database replication for disaster recovery?
informatics.a: Real-time synchronized copy ready for immediate failover
c: Real-time synchronized copy ready for immediate failover
Backup that requires hours of restore time to deploy
Monthly database export saved to external USB drive
Cold storage archive on tape kept in an offsite vault
